commit.message:
feat(redesign): premium visual overhaul, number reconciliation, performance fix

Visual:
- Hero carousel: SVG chevron arrows (w-12 h-12), gradient CTA buttons
- Typography: eliminated all sub-10px text, minimum now text-xs (12px)
- Sub-pages: unique color-identity hero banners (cyan/mercado, green/tech, purple/pesquisa, red/seguranca)
- Panorama: larger KPIs, larger section headers, premium glass styling

Numbers reconciled:
- Countries: 972→37 (filter ≥20 jobs, exclude garbage)
- Insights: 104→46 (only publishable)
- Sources: 85→36 (real count)
- Removed fake 'Capital Rastreado US$ 1.4T' KPI (funding data is mis-sourced)
- Replaced with 'Tech Trends' KPI using real tech_trends data (38K signals)
- Fixed '~85% EUA' to '~54% EUA'
- Patents filtered: 37K mock-generated excluded

Methodology page:
- Replaced fake source list (CrunchBase, PitchBook, LinkedIn API) with real connected sources
- Added REAL/MOCK badges per KPI (10 real, 10 mock)
- Added honesty disclaimer about unconnected sources

Performance:
- Created materialized view mv_papers_vs_jobs (85s query→<100ms)
- Charts API now returns in <1s instead of timing out

Panorama charts:
- Replaced empty 'Investimento por Tipo' panel with 'Geopolítica ACLED' (929K events)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/fe063606-4785-4a11-ba16-6dcbdaaa7c80

* Drop Python 3.8 support: incompatible with setuptools>=78.1.1

setuptools 78.x requires Python >= 3.9, so building for Python 3.8
fails after the setuptools minimum was raised to 78.1.1. Remove 3.8
from the CI test matrix and all release scripts (Linux, macOS, Windows),
and update the twine upload environments from py3.8 to py3.9.

---------

commit.message:
Add codex-instructions.md and install user instructions

Create codex-instructions.md as the Codex CLI equivalent of
copilot-instructions.md, with Azure DevOps section removed.
Both install.sh and install.ps1 now copy user-level instruction
files to ~/.copilot/ and ~/.codex/ during default installs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add cross-platform prerequisite install instructions to all READMEs

- Add Prerequisites table to top-level README with winget/apt/brew commands
- Add expandable install instructions to circuit-sim, elmer-fem, netlist-to-schematic READMEs
- Clarify ParaView is optional (GUI-only, not invoked programmatically)
- Update SKILL.md prerequisite sections for cross-platform clarity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Polish README: fix grammar, jargon, passive voice, and descriptions

- 'not time to become' → 'who don't have time to become'
- 'items deployed' → 'everything deployed' (avoid Fabric jargon)
- 'the architecture' → 'an architecture' (13 task flows exist)
- Deploy row: passive → active voice to match other rows
- 'project-brief' → 'project brief' + em-dash for rhythm
- Item Registry: describe what the reader finds, not what the agent needs
- Templates: 'definition files' not 'starting points' (they're the real thing)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: fabric-task-flows
repository.description: Task flows let you describe your problem and have an AI agent generate your Microsoft Fabric solution—workspaces, pipelines, notebooks, data agents, models, reports, and more—delivered as a CI/CD‑ready script, ready to deploy.

commit.message:
Fix circuit-sim for Windows: text rawfile + encoding

- parse_rawfile.py: Support both binary and text (Values:) rawfile
  formats. Windows ngspice outputs text format even with -r flag.
- run_sim.py: Add encoding='utf-8' to all NamedTemporaryFile and
  Path.read_text() calls. Fixes UnicodeEncodeError on Windows
  where the default encoding is cp1252.

Tested: all three example netlists (rc_lowpass, pulse_response,
rc_sweep) pass end-to-end on Windows with ngspice 45.2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Windows/PowerShell cross-platform support

- Add install.ps1 for circuit-sim, elmer-fem, netlist-to-schematic
  (modeled after memory's existing install.ps1)
- Add top-level install.ps1 that iterates skills/*/install.ps1
- Add .gitattributes enforcing LF for .sh, CRLF for .ps1
- Update all READMEs with Windows PowerShell installation instructions
- Fix elmer-fem SKILL.md: replace sed/inline env var with cross-platform
- Fix memory SKILL.md: remove bash-specific mkdir -p and date syntax
- Fix AGENTS.md /tmp/ paths with relative paths (cross-platform)
- Add explicit encoding='utf-8' to run_sim.py Path.read_text()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Rewrite README for a warmer first impression

- Lead with bold value prop instead of dry definition
- Add 'Who is this for?' section so readers self-identify
- Reframe skills table as benefits ('What it does for you')
- Move Quick Start below the pitch (why before how)
- Fold Item Registry and Templates into What's Inside table
- Consistent conversational tone throughout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Deep scrub: dead code removal, doc fixes, skill efficiency, registry+signal updates

Wave 1 - Dead code & doc fixes:
- Remove orphaned $cli_version lines from sync-item-types.py
- Remove deprecated build_fab_commands() from registry_loader.py + tests
- Replace fragile importlib hack in fleet-runner.py with text_utils.slugify
- Fix stale counts in README (45 items, 838 tests), CONTRIBUTING, CHANGELOG
- Update fabric-test SKILL.md to 3-state validation (passed/partial/failed)

Wave 2 - Skill efficiency:
- Consolidate fabric-discover Steps 4/5 to eliminate duplicate user Q&A
- Add parallel-read instruction to fabric-document Step 1

Wave 3 - Registry & signal mapper:
- Add Scorecard item type to registry (45 total items)
- Patch signal-categories.json with 8 new keywords across 3 categories
- Add low-coverage advisory to signal-mapper.py output
- Align task type names in taskflow-gen.py, tests, and schema (ingest/store/etc)
- Fix test-plan-prefill.py availability check to match registry abbreviation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Clean typography: single sans-serif font, solid divider, allergies always shown

- Single font (system-ui sans-serif) throughout
- 3 sizes only: 0.75rem labels, 0.85rem message, 0.9rem values
- Weight used for hierarchy: 700 labels, 600 names, 400 values
- Solid vertical divider between left/star, inset from top/bottom
- Allergies field always visible (shows dash if empty)
- Dashed cut lines between cards, no outer border

- Serif labels (Georgia) for a polished print feel
- Inline label: value layout with natural wrapping
- Allergies only shown when relevant (not 'no'/'none')
- Message in italic serif below To/From with subtle separator

- Clean B&W print output, 4 per page

commit.message:
feat: multi-storage support and Microsoft DI integration

- Extract IStoreTransaction interface from LmdbTransaction
- Update IMessageStore to use IStoreTransaction instead of LmdbTransaction
- Move LMDB storage implementation to separate Bielu.PersistentQueues.Storage.LMDB project
- Remove LightningDB dependency from core project
- Add Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.Abstractions to core project
- Create ServiceCollectionExtensions with PersistentQueuesBuilder for MS DI
- Create LmdbServiceCollectionExtensions for LMDB storage DI registration
- Update QueueConfiguration.WithDefaults() to remove LMDB-specific setup
- Update solution file, test project, and benchmark project references

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix 5 misclassified task_type values in item registry

govern was used as a catch-all for newer items. Reclassified:
- VariableLibrary: govern -> develop (legend already listed it as develop)
- DataAgent: govern -> distribute (exposes data via conversational AI)
- GraphModel: govern -> prepare (models data into graph structures)
- GraphQuerySet: govern -> prepare (saved graph queries, like KQLQueryset)
- OperationsAgent: govern -> track (monitors operations, like Reflex)

Only Ontology correctly remains as govern (metadata governance).
Updated \ legend descriptions to match.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
